input.subject: Checking on refund timeline
input.message: Hi, I returned the wireless keyboard five days ago and the tracking page says it was delivered to your warehouse on Monday. I still have not seen the refund on my credit card. Can you let me know when I should expect it?
input.account_context: Order #48192. Return delivered 5 days ago. Standard policy says refunds are processed within 7 business days after warehouse receipt.
input.policy_notes: Do not promise an exact bank posting date. Give the normal processing window and offer to follow up if it exceeds policy.

good_output_should: Clearly explain the 7-business-day window, reassure the customer, avoid promising an exact date, and offer next steps if the refund does not appear after the policy window.

input.subject: I was charged twice
input.message: This is ridiculous. I was charged twice for the same subscription renewal and nobody has replied for two days. I want one of the charges reversed immediately and I need an explanation.
input.account_context: Subscription renewal on March 28. Two charges appear in billing system, but one may be an authorization hold. No prior agent response has been sent.
input.policy_notes: Do not admit fault before confirming the duplicate charge. Acknowledge frustration, explain that one charge may be temporary, and state that billing will investigate.

good_output_should: Use calm empathetic language, avoid blaming or overpromising, explain the possible authorization-hold scenario, and commit to investigation with a realistic timeline.

input.subject: Need a refund after 45 days
input.message: I bought your annual plan 45 days ago and forgot to cancel. I barely used it. Can you make an exception and refund it?
input.account_context: Annual plan activated 45 days ago. Refund policy allows cancellations within 14 days of purchase.
input.policy_notes: Do not promise a refund. You may acknowledge the request, explain the published policy, and offer a downgrade or cancellation of future renewal.

good_output_should: Politely explain the 14-day policy, avoid promising an exception, and offer a practical alternative such as canceling renewal or exploring a lower-cost plan.

input.subject: Locked out after password reset
input.message: I reset my password and now I cannot get into my account before an investor demo in an hour. Please fix this ASAP.
input.account_context: No verified account ownership steps completed yet. No device or security log information available.
input.policy_notes: Never suggest bypassing identity verification. Ask for the approved verification steps and direct the customer to secure recovery flow.

good_output_should: Show urgency and empathy, but avoid unsafe recovery advice, request the approved verification process, and avoid pretending the issue can be fixed immediately without validation.

input.subject: Delete my data now
input.message: Delete all of my data immediately. If this is not done today, I will report your company and contact my lawyer.
input.account_context: User requests account deletion. Jurisdiction and account verification status unknown.
input.policy_notes: Do not cite laws unless provided. Acknowledge the request, explain that identity verification is required before account deletion, and escalate privacy requests to the privacy team.

good_output_should: Remain calm, avoid legal claims, explain verification and escalation clearly, and avoid promising same-day deletion without the required process.

input.subject: Please add CSV export
input.message: Our team really needs CSV export from the dashboard. Right now we have to copy everything manually. Is this on your roadmap?
input.account_context: No CSV export feature currently available. Product team accepts feature requests through internal tracker. No committed launch date.
input.policy_notes: Do not promise roadmap dates. Thank the customer, summarize the request, and offer a workaround if available.

good_output_should: Acknowledge the request, avoid inventing a roadmap date, offer a workaround if possible, and note that feedback will be shared with the product team.
